INTERNATIONAL RELATIONS EVENTS & VISITS
INTERNATIONAL RELATIONS EVENTS & VISITS
The Centre for International Relations (CIR) at Sri Eshwar highlights key global collaborations, student exchange programs, and leadership visits. This section showcases initiatives that strengthen ties with international institutions and promote global learning opportunities.
EVENTS
2025-26
2024-25
S.No.
Date
Name of Session
Descriptions
1
25.02.2026
Interactive Session with Public Service Aspirants
The Interactive Session with Public Service Aspirants was successfully conducted, providing valuable guidance to students preparing for TNPSC and other government examinations.
The session featured P. Senthil Kumar, Deputy Tahsildar, Revenue Department, Namakkal, and A.K. Kavitha, Assistant, Public Works Department, Namakkal, who shared their preparation strategies, experiences, and insights into public service careers.
Students actively interacted with the speakers, clarified their doubts, and gained practical direction for their preparation. The session was informative, inspiring, and highly beneficial for all aspiring candidates.
2
12.02.026
Virtal MoU Signing Ceremony with Cavite State University
Philippines
The Virtual MoU Signing Ceremony between Sri Eshwar College of Engineering and Cavite State University, Philippines was successfully held on 12th February 2026. The event marked the formalization of academic collaboration between the two institutions, aiming to promote student exchange, faculty collaboration, joint research, and international academic initiatives. The ceremony was presided over by the Vice President of Cavite State University and the Principal of Sri Eshwar College of Engineering, in the presence of Heads of Departments, faculty members, and the Centre for International Relations (CIR) teams from both institutions. This partnership signifies a strong step toward fostering global academic cooperation and cross-border educational opportunities.
3
12.02.2026
International Seminar on AI for a Sustainabe Energy Future
A Virtual International Guest Lecture was delivered by Dr. N. Pushpalatha and Dr. D. Gunapriya on the topic “AI for Smart Energy Management for Smart Cities” as part of the International Seminar on “AI for a Sustainable Energy Future” held on 12th February 2026. The session highlighted the transformative role of Artificial Intelligence in optimizing energy systems, enhancing efficiency, and supporting sustainable urban development. The lecture provided valuable insights into innovative AI-driven solutions for smart cities and encouraged participants to explore advanced research and practical applications in sustainable energy management.
4
4,5,6.02.2026
Guest lecture on "From Code to Customer" by International Faculty Members
A Guest Lecture titled “From Code to Customer” was delivered by Ms.Haslenna Hamdan, Senior Lecturer & International Liaison Officer, Marketing Department, Faculty of Business & Management, UiTM Terengganu Branch, Malaysia, as part of the International Visiting Faculty initiative at Sri Eshwar College of Engineering. The session provided valuable insights into bridging the gap between technical development and market success, emphasizing the importance of customer-centric strategies, innovation, and effective marketing in transforming ideas into impactful solutions. The lecture offered students a global perspective on aligning technology with business objectives and enhanced their understanding of industry-oriented practices.
5
4,5,6.02.2026
Participation of International Students & Faculty members from Russia and Malaysia in different events & Competitions
Thiran 2026 – Confluence of Talents was a week-long international academic and cultural exchange programme hosted at Sri Eshwar College of Engineering, Coimbatore, where international students and faculty from Russia and Malaysia actively participated in technical workshops, competitions, interactive academic sessions, and the International Education Fair. The visit also included curated local cultural tours and heritage experiences, providing participants with meaningful exposure to Indian traditions, fostering global collaboration, cross-cultural learning, and lasting institutional partnerships.
6
10.11.2025
Higher Studies, Research, and Work Opportunities in Germany
The Collaborative Discussion on Study and Work Opportunities in Germany is an interactive session featuring Dr. Dirk Fröhling, Professor, Westphalian University of Applied Sciences; Dr. Carolin Radscheit, Professor, Bochum University of Applied Sciences; Dr. Ralf Förster, Professor, Berlin University of Applied Sciences and Technology; Dr. Jörg Frochte, Professor, Bochum University of Applied Sciences; and Mr. Nils Daniel Wiese, Program Manager, IGCHE Germany, focusing on higher education pathways, applied research opportunities, scholarships, internships, and work prospects in Germany, while exploring academic collaboration and student mobility initiative
7
30.10.2025
Study and Work Opportunities in Dubai
The Study and Work Opportunities in Dubai event is an informative session featuring Mr. Vipul (Dubai) and Mr. Bharath from Meta Apply, aimed at providing students with insights into higher education options, career pathways, internships, and employment prospects in Dubai, along with guidance on admissions, visas, and global career planning.
8
10.09.2025
Academic and Employment Prospects in Denmark: An Interactive Dialogue
The Collaborative Discussion on Study and Work Opportunities in Denmark is an interactive session featuring Ms. Sarah Gram, Professor and International Student Recruitment Manager, Aarhus University, Denmark; Ms. Helle Yding Sørensen, International Student Recruitment Manager, University of Southern Denmark; and Ms. Christabel Royan, Director, Nordic Centre in India, focusing on higher education options, scholarships, internships, work opportunities, and academic collaboration prospects in Denmark and the Nordic region.
9
26.08.2025
Exploring Higher Education and Career Pathways at Shibarua Institute of Technology in Japan
The Collaborative Discussion on Study and Work Opportunities in Japan is an interactive session led by Ms. Uma Maheswari Rajagopalan, Professor, Department of Innovative Program, Shibaura Institute of Technology, Tokyo, Japan, focusing on higher education pathways, research opportunities, scholarships, internships, and work prospects in Japan, while exploring possibilities for academic collaboration and student exchange initiatives.
10
08.08.2025
University of Canterbury Delegates Visit Sri Eshwar College of Engineering
University of Canterbury Delegates Visit Sri Eshwar College of Engineering, in association with Placeme Consultancy Services, Coimbatore, aims to strengthen international academic collaboration by providing insights into study opportunities, programs, scholarships, and career pathways at the University of Canterbury, while offering students valuable guidance on overseas education and global career prospects.
11
05.08.2025
Study and Work Opportunities in Taiwan
The Collaborative Discussion on Study and Work Opportunities in Taiwan is an interactive session led by Dr.Khire Rushikesh Ulhas, Assistant Professor, International Business Administration Program, Providence University, Taiwan, aimed at providing insights into higher education opportunities, scholarships, internships, and work prospects in Taiwan, while also exploring avenues for academic collaboration and student mobility between institutions.
12
23.07.2025 - 04.08.2025
Two Weeks International Internship Program - Valedictory Function
The Two Weeks International Internship Program – Valedictory Function marks the successful completion of the program and features Mr. Lakshmanan LS, Manager – Campus Hiring, VVDN Technologies, Coimbatore, and Mr. Hiroyuki Ishida, Founder, Mosaique Private Limited, Bangalore, who will share insights on industry expectations, global internship experiences, and career readiness.
13
01.08.2025
Two Weeks International Internship Program
Session 7 on "AI for Supply Chain & Logistics Optimization"
The Two Weeks International Internship Program – Session 7 on “AI for Supply Chain & Logistics Optimization” features Ms. E. Anitha, Assistant Professor, Department of Artificial Intelligence and Data Science, Sri Eshwar College of Engineering, Coimbatore, who will discuss the application of artificial intelligence in enhancing efficiency, decision-making, and optimization across modern supply chain and logistics systems.
14
31.07.2025
Two Weeks International Internship Program
Session 6 on "Unlocking the Unseen Strategic Business transformation using Gen AI
"
The Two Weeks International Internship Program – Session 6 on “Unlocking the Unseen Strategic Business Transformation Using Gen AI” features Mr. Ragavendran Chandrasekaran from Cargill, who will share insights on how generative AI is driving strategic innovation, decision-making, and transformative change in modern business environments.
15
29.07.2025
Two Weeks International Internship Program -
Session 4 on "AI in Marketing: Predicting Customer Behavior"
The Two Weeks International Internship Program – Session 4 on “AI in Marketing: Predicting Customer Behavior” features Ms. V. Sangavi, Assistant Professor, Department of Artificial Intelligence and Data Science, and Mr. K. P. Siva, Assistant Professor, Department of Computer Science and Business Systems, Sri Eshwar College of Engineering, Coimbatore, who will discuss how AI-driven models and analytics are used to understand, predict, and influence customer behavior in modern marketing strategies.
16
28.07.2025
Two Weeks International Internship Program
Session 3 on "Design Thinking for Business Innovation"
The Two Weeks International Internship Program – Session 3 on “Design Thinking for Business Innovation” features Mr. N. Vishnu Sakaravarthy, Assistant Professor, Department of Mechanical Engineering, and Mr. M. Vivek Kumar, Assistant Professor, Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering, Sri Eshwar College of Engineering, Coimbatore, who will explore design thinking principles, creative problem-solving approaches, and innovation-driven strategies for business applications.
17
25.07.2025
Two Weeks International Internship Program
Session 2 on "Business Intelligence with AI Tools (Power BI, Tableau)''
The Two Weeks International Internship Program – Session 2 on “Business Intelligence with AI Tools (Power BI, Tableau)” features Dr. K. Kathiresan, Assistant Professor, Department of AI & ML, Sri Eshwar College of Engineering, Coimbatore, who will provide insights into data-driven decision-making using modern BI tools integrated with AI techniques.
18
24.07.2025
Two Weeks International Internship Program
Session 1 on "Introduction to AI for Business "
The Two Weeks International Internship Program – Session 1 on “Introduction to AI for Business” features Mr. Shiridi Sai Shanmugam, Senior Product Analyst, Aventra Group, Coimbatore, who will introduce the fundamentals of artificial intelligence and its practical applications in driving business insights, efficiency, and strategic decision-making.
19
23.07.2025
Two Weeks International Internship Program - Inaguration
The Two Weeks International Internship Program – Inauguration features Mr. Mohit Bansal, AI Solutions Architect, Helios Core, Hyderabad, who will set the context for the program by sharing insights on emerging AI trends, industry expectations, and the role of AI in shaping future business and technology landscapes.
20
15.07.2025
Leadership Program about Japan Culture and work opportunities with Japanese Delegates visit Sri Eshwar College Of Engineering
The Leadership Program on Japanese Culture and Work Opportunities, conducted during the visit of Japanese delegates to Sri Eshwar College of Engineering, features Mr. Tomio Isogai, Advisor, Indobox Inc., Japan; Ms. Mariko Hanaoka, Co-founder & Director, Indobox Inc., Japan; and Ms. Tomoko Yuasa, Partner, Indobox Inc., Japan, who will share insights on Japanese work culture, leadership values, industry expectations, and career opportunities in Japan for aspiring global professionals.
21
18.06.2025
Discussion with Australia Delegates Visit Sri Eshwar College Of Engineering
S.No
Date
Name of the Event
Descriptions
1
17.04.2025
Maynooth University Delegates Visit Sri Eshwar College Of Engineering
Delegates from Maynooth University, Ireland visited Sri Eshwar College of Engineering as part of strengthening international collaborations. They were welcomed by Dr. Sudha Mohanram, Principal, and received a college overview from Mr. Kannan Naranshimhan, Dean – International and Industry Relations. The visit included a campus tour and discussions on future partnerships.
2
17.03.2025
TNPSC training
TNPSC training has commenced for first, second, and third-year students across all departments, led by experienced external trainers. The programme includes focused sessions on general studies, aptitude, and current affairs, along with regular practice tests and updated study materials—aligned with the latest TNPSC exam pattern and syllabus.
3
15.03.2025
MIRAI Event
The Centre for International Relations at Sri Eshwar College of Engineering organized MIRAI 2025, an intercollege event exclusively for first-year students. The event featured a variety of technical, cultural, and creative competitions aimed at encouraging talent, teamwork, and global perspectives.
MIRAI provided a vibrant platform for students to showcase their skills and connect with peers from other institutions.
4
5
08.03.2025
GATE training
GATE coaching has begun for II‑year students of Electronics & Communication, Electrical & Electronics and Mechanical Engineering, led by seasoned external experts. The programme features focused concept lectures, regular mock tests and practice sessions, and curated study materials—all aligned with the latest GATE syllabus.
6
03.03.2025
IELTS Master Class
The Centre for International Relations (CIR) successfully hosted an IELTS Master Class in collaboration with IDP Education. This session provided valuable insights into the IELTS test format, scoring criteria, and essential strategies to achieve a high band score.
7
26.02.2025
Collaborative Discussion on Study and Work Opportunities in Japan
Sri Eshwar College of Engineering, in collaboration with KC Consulting Services, Coimbatore, discusssed about study and career prospects in Japan. Experts provided insights on admissions, scholarships, internships, and job opportunities, guiding students toward global career pathways.
8
22.02.2025
International Education Expo at Thiran 2025
As part of Thiran 2025, the Centre for International Relations (CIR) organized an International Education Expo, where 50+ aspiring students explored global study opportunities. The event featured esteemed guests, Ms. Deepa, Head of Operations, IDP India, and Ms. Shruthi, Manager, Campus France, Embassy of France, who provided valuable insights on studying abroad, admissions, and scholarships.
9
20.02.2025
University of Essex Delegates Visit Sri Eshwar College of Engineering
Delegates from the University of Essex visited Sri Eshwar College of Engineering, Coimbatore, to explore various opportunities for collaboration between the two institutions. The discussions focused on academic partnerships, student exchange programs, and research initiatives to strengthen international engagement.
10
18.02.2025
Promoting UK Culture – Digital Poster Competition
The CIR team organized a digital poster competition showcasing UK culture and technological advancements to commemorate the visit of UK delegates from the University of Essex. The event encouraged creativity and cross-cultural learning among students.
11
14.02.2025
Discussion with Regional Officer from De Montfort University - UCOL
Ms. Kousalya, an International Education Counselor, successfully facilitated a meeting with the Regional Officer from De Montfort University - UCOL. The session focused on academic collaborations, study opportunities, and admission pathways, providing valuable insights for students aspiring to pursue higher education at De Montfort University.
12
13.02.2025,
14.02.2025
Passport Campaign Benefiting 400 Students
A Passport Campaign was organized on two dates, benefiting 400 students. Ms. Kanagalatha, Senior International Relationship Coordinator, successfully facilitated the event, ensuring smooth application support and guidance for students pursuing international opportunities.
13
12.02.2025
Discussion with University Relationship Manager from Anglia Ruskin University - UCOL
Ms. Kousalya, an International Education Counselor, facilitated a meeting with the University Relationship Manager from Anglia Ruskin University - UCO. The session focused on exploring academic collaborations, study opportunities, and potential pathways for students to pursue higher education at Anglia Ruskin University.
14
12.02.2025
Interactive Session with GATE-Registered Students at Sri Eshwar
Mr. Kannan Narashimhan and Dr. Karthikeyan provided valuable guidance on exam preparation strategies and highlighted the various support programs available, including training sessions and resource materials, to help students excel in the GATE examination
15
07.02.2025
HEC Aspirants' Meeting with CIR
Mr. Kannan Narashimhan and Dr. Karthikeyan from the Centre for International Relations (CIR) organized a meeting for students interested in HEC, providing insights into the support and training programs offered by CIR to assist them in their academic pursuits.
16
29.01.2025
Study in Japan: Academic and Cultural Insights
Ms. Mairi, representing the Consulate General of Japan from the Embassy of Japan, visited the campus to explore opportunities for pursuing master's programs in Japan and to share insights into its vibrant cultural experiences.
17
26.12.2025
Job opportunities in Japan
Ms. Mariko Hanaoka, Co-founder and Director of Indobox, visited our campus and engaged in an insightful interaction with Japanese language students, discussing exciting job opportunities in Japan.
18
17.12.2024
German Universities Collaboration
Visited by German delegates, including Prof. Dr. Christian Werner, CEO of Hochschule der Wirtschaft für Management, along with his team. The visit focused on exploring potential opportunities for collaborative programs with the management team.
19
11.12.2024
San Diego State University USA
Leadership team exchanged an MoU and explored further academic and research tie-ups to strengthen institutional relations
20
04.12.2024
Durham University UK
Professors from Durham University highlighted master’s programmes and discussed collaboration
21
04.12.2024
Promoting UK Culture – Drawing Competition
In honor of Durham University delegates visiting Sri Eshwar College of Engineering, the CIR Team organized a drawing competition celebrating UK culture. This initiative fostered creativity and cultural appreciation among students.
22
24.09.2024
Harare Institute of Technology, Zimbabwe
Formal signing of MoU by the Vice-Chancellor and esteemed professors to establish partnerships
